observed: M-012 explicitly handed off a report-only repository inventory.
The existing loader normalizes continuity records and validates inherited unresolved claims.
The existing fork detector preserves historical forks rather than rewriting them.
The new inventory composer and four deterministic checks are committed.
inferred: A report can be valid while retaining multiple historical heads or forks, provided malformed records, missing dependencies, and dropped unresolved claims are reported rather than concealed.
proposed: Add repository path discovery so every operations/queue-updates/M-*.json record can be supplied automatically to this report.
superseded: Treating a single current head as a required acceptance condition for historical continuity reporting.
unresolved: repository-wide automatic discovery of every M-* queue record
classification of intentional versus accidental forks
semantic matching for differently worded but equivalent unresolved claims

verification.method: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical source and test content

privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata, abstract decision structure, and synthetic queue records only. Raw private chat, health and pet-health material, credentials, identifying personal information, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
limits: No repository directory-discovery API was used in this slice, so the artifact composes supplied entries but does not prove every historical M-* record was discovered.
No exact-commit CI run was observed.
A structurally valid inventory does not establish the truth of every historical claim.
